tool.name: Yep Gmail
tool.summary: Read, search, label, and send Gmail on the user's behalf via the Gmail API. MCP-stdio server, OAuth refresh-token auth, no inbox-content stored server-side.
tool.description:
Exposes a small MCP toolset over the Gmail API: search_threads, get_thread, create_draft, label_thread, unlabel_thread, list_drafts, list_labels, create_label. Designed for autonomous agents that need to triage, draft replies, and organize a user's inbox without a human in the loop. Authenticates via standard Google OAuth installed-app flow; the user grants once, the refresh token is stored locally by the host, and the tool exchanges it for short-lived access tokens at runtime.

No message bodies are persisted by the tool itself. All API calls flow user → tool → Google → tool → user.

env.name: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID
env.prompt: Your Google OAuth 2.0 Client ID for the project that has the Gmail API enabled. Looks like '123456789012-abc...apps.googleusercontent.com'. Found in Google Cloud Console > APIs & Services > Credentials.

env.name: GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET
env.prompt: Your Google OAuth 2.0 Client Secret, paired with the Client ID above. Treat as a password.

env.name: GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN
env.prompt: Long-lived OAuth refresh token for the Gmail account this tool will operate on. Run `python -m yep_gmail_tool.authorize` once to obtain — follow the URL, paste the consent code, and the token prints. The host stores it in your secret backend.

env.name: GMAIL_DEFAULT_LABEL
env.prompt: Optional default Gmail label this tool will apply to messages it processes. Leave blank to skip auto-labeling. Useful for distinguishing agent-touched threads from human-touched ones in your inbox.

scopes.rationale: Required to search the user's inbox, fetch threads, and read message bodies for context when drafting replies or triaging.
Creates draft replies for the user to review and send; lists existing drafts to avoid duplicates. Drafts are never auto-sent by this tool.

Applies and removes labels on threads to mark agent-processed messages and to support user-defined organization schemes. Cannot read message contents under this scope alone — works alongside gmail.messages:read.
Tool calls api.googleapis.com for all Gmail operations. No other outbound destinations.
